behavior_summary: Provides examine ('U8P_LOGO::look') and use behaviors for a logo/item. When examined, the script chooses a short descriptive prefix based on the item's frame (variant) and asks the item to 'bark' that string to the viewer.

When used, the script sequences spawned effects oriented to the actor and two short dialog barks from the item. It spawns a general effect, queries the actor's facing direction, spawns a direction-dependent effect, barks a first line, spawns a second direction-dependent effect, then barks a concluding line.
Frame thresholds partition descriptors into three categories: 'magical sphere ', 'magical tetrahedron ', and 'magical cube '.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LOGO::LOGO::look calls Item::getFrame(item) and branches on the returned frame value to pick one of three descriptor strings, then calls Item::bark(item, string) and frees the temporary string.
U8P_LOGO::LOGO::use runs a sequence of spawn and wait/suspend operations interleaved with calls to Npc::getDir(pid) to obtain orientation and Item::bark(item, string) for two dialog lines.
Use-handler relies on suspension points (waits) between spawns and barks to sequence effects and dialog, implying temporal ordering rather than instantaneous multi-spawn.
open_questions: Exact semantics and visual/functional effect of spawn METHOD::091C and METHOD::15E7 (inferred to be visual/timed effects but implementation details are unknown).
Precise meaning of the numeric frame thresholds (why <6, 6–10, >=11) and whether they map to art assets or gameplay tiers.
The callback/addr parameters passed to spawn calls are visible only as addresses/values in the disassembly — their runtime behavior and lifetime are not fully recoverable here.
Whether LOGO::use is triggered by a specific event code (inferred Event 1) or other interaction types in the engine's event dispatcher.

main_function.name: U8P_LOGO::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the item's examine/U8P_LOGO::look action: determine the item's visual variant (frame) and present a short descriptive prefix by invoking Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0: item is 'looked at' / examined (actor triggered the item's U8P_LOGO::look event)

function_map.name: U8P_LOGO::look
function_map.role: Examine handler that selects a descriptor based on Item::getFrame and calls Item::bark with that string.

function_map.name: U8P_LOGO::use
function_map.role: Use handler that spawns effects (METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7), queries actor direction (Npc::getDir), and emits two dialog barks via Item::bark while sequencing waits.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Return the item's frame/variant index so logic can pick the descriptor.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit or queue a text/dialog line associated with the item (used for descriptors and spoken lines).

function_map.name: Npc::getDir
function_map.role: Return facing direction for an actor/pid so spawned effects can be oriented.

function_map.name: METHOD::091C
function_map.role: Spawned engine routine; likely creates a timed or attachment effect tied to pid (exact effect inferred).

function_map.name: METHOD::15E7
function_map.role: Direction-dependent spawn routine called with effect codes (0x31, 0x32); likely draws orientation-specific visual effects.

external_calls.purpose: Retrieve the item's frame/variant index to decide which descriptor string to present.
Make the item 'speak' the chosen descriptor to the examining actor/process.
Spawn a general effect attached to the actor/pid before directional effects (likely a non-directional visual/timed effect).
Get the actor's facing direction so subsequent directional effects can be oriented correctly.
Spawn a direction-dependent effect using the direction from Npc::getDir and an effect code (called twice with codes 0x31 and 0x32).
Emit two short dialog lines from the item interleaved with effect spawns to convey narrative flavor ('I have not the strength...' and '...but one day I shall!').

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides examine ('U8P_LOGO::look') and use behaviors for a logo/item. When examined, the script chooses a short descriptive prefix based on the item's frame (variant) and asks the item to 'bark' that string to the viewer.
